case_summary: A complaint has been filed with the Haryana Police, under the Explosive Act, 1884, and an FIR has been registered. The complainant, Gurmel Singh, has reported an incident that occurred in village Rana Majra, Beat No. 4, PS Sanoli, Panipat.

sections.title: Theft
sections.description: Whoever, intending to take dishonestly any property out of the possession of any person without that person's consent, moves that property with the intention of taking it out of India or with the intention of permanently depriving that person of that property, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three years, or with fine, or with both.

sop.action: Register FIR immediately under BNSS Section 173 - this is a cognizable offence
sop.file_url: sop/Regulation No. 14 of 2024.GUIDLINE FOR EXPEDITIOUS  & FAIR INVESTIGATION OF RAPE CASES.pdf

sc_judgments.case_name: Lalita Kumari vs Govt. of UP (2013)
sc_judgments.guideline: FIR must be registered immediately on receipt of cognizable offense
sc_judgments.holding: The Constitution Bench unanimously held that registration of FIR is mandatory under Section 154 CrPC (now BNSS 173) if the information discloses commission of a cognizable offence.
key_points: FIR cannot be refused on any pretext for cognizable offences
Preliminary inquiry can only be done in cases like matrimonial disputes, commercial cases, medical negligence, corruption - not in serious crimes
Officer refusing to register FIR is liable under departmental action and contempt of court
Victim can approach SP or Magistrate if SHO refuses
sc_judgments.io_duty: Mandatory - Register FIR immediately. No preliminary inquiry for cognizable offences. Non-compliance invites disciplinary action.

evidence_checklist: Seize any explosives or related materials
Record witness statements under BNSS Sec 180
io_warnings: MANDATORY: Do not refuse FIR - Lalita Kumari SC judgment applies
MANDATORY: Arrest must follow D.K. Basu SC guidelines
